seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with BeeAI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with BeeAI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI Chatbots with BeeAI enables robust conversational agents using IBM's open-source framework, featuring memory, tools, and observability for production-grade deployments. Guardrails are essential to address failure modes like unbounded memory growth, tool injection vulnerabilities, state serialization errors, and model output inconsistencies. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, coherence, safety, PII protection, and hallucination detection in high-risk chatbot scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with BeeAI
architectureOverview.description:
The BeeAI AI Chatbot follows a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pattern with intent classification and multi-turn memory. User input triggers intent classification via a Granite model, followed by vector database retrieval for relevant context. The agent maintains conversation memory across turns, invokes tools for external actions, and generates responses with safety filtering.

Key components include BeeAI's Agent class for orchestration, MemoryManager for session state, ToolRegistry for function calling, and Granite LLM integration for reasoning. Observability hooks capture events for monitoring and verification. This architecture handles complex multi-turn conversations while requiring strict guardrails for production reliability.

Vex integrates as an event listener to verify agent outputs against accuracy, coherence, safety, PII, and hallucination criteria before user delivery.

risks.name: Unbounded Memory Growth
risks.description: Agent memory accumulates indefinitely in long conversations, causing OOM errors and degraded performance
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors memory size per session and enforces truncation thresholds

risks.name: Tool Injection Vulnerabilities
risks.description: Malicious inputs bypass tool validation, enabling unauthorized function execution
risks.mitigation: Vex validates tool parameters and sanitizes inputs before execution

risks.name: PII Leakage in Responses
risks.description: Chatbot exposes sensitive user data in generated responses or tool calls
risks.mitigation: Vex scans outputs for PII patterns and applies redaction/blocking

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG Context
risks.description: Agent generates factually incorrect responses despite retrieved context
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies response fidelity to retrieved documents and sources

implementation.title: Basic BeeAI Chatbot Setup
implementation.description: Initialize BeeAI agent with memory, tools, and Granite LLM for conversational RAG
implementation.code:
from beeai import Agent, MemoryManager, ToolRegistry
from beeai.llms import GraniteLLM

# Initialize components
llm = GraniteLLM(model='granite-3b-chat')
memory = MemoryManager(max_tokens=8000)
tools = ToolRegistry()

agent = Agent(
    llm=llm,
    memory=memory,
    tools=tools,
    system_prompt="You are a helpful chatbot assistant."
)

# Register RAG tool
@tools.register
def search_knowledge_base(query: str) -> str:
    # Vector DB retrieval logic
    return 'Retrieved context about ' + query

implementation.title: Intent Classification Pipeline
implementation.description: Classify user intent and route to appropriate agent behavior

implementation.title: Multi-turn Memory Management
implementation.description: Persist conversation state with automatic summarization for long sessions
implementation.code:
def process_conversation(user_input: str) -> str:
    # Add to memory
    memory.add_user_message(user_input)
    
    # Check memory size
    if memory.token_count > 6000:
        summary = llm.summarize(memory.get_history())
        memory.reset(summary)
    
    # Generate response
    response = agent.run(user_input)
    memory.add_assistant_message(response)
    
    return response

# Usage
response = process_conversation('What is RAG?')

implementation.title: Safety Filtering Wrapper
implementation.description: Basic content filtering before response delivery
implementation.code:
import re

def safe_generate(user_input: str) -> str:
    # Block harmful content
    if re.search(r'\b(?:kill|bomb|hack)\b', user_input, re.IGNORECASE):
        return "I can't help with that request."
    
    response = agent.run(user_input)
    
    # Block PII patterns
    pii_patterns = [r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b', r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b']
    for pattern in pii_patterns:
        response = re.sub(pattern, '[REDACTED]', response)
    
    return response

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Event Listener
vexIntegration.description: Hook Vex into BeeAI's event system for comprehensive verification
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from beeai.events import EventBus

# Initialize Vex client
vex = VexClient(api_key='your-vex-api-key')

class VexListener(EventListener):
    def __init__(self):
        self.verifications = [
            'response_accuracy',
            'conversation_coherence',
            'safety_filtering',
            'pii_protection',
            'hallucination_detection'
        ]
    
    def on_agent_response(self, response_data):
        verdict = vex.verify(
            agent_output=response_data['response'],
            conversation_history=response_data['history'],
            verifications=self.verifications,
            risk_profile='high'
        )
        
        if not verdict.approved:
            return "[VEX BLOCKED: " + verdict.reason + "]"
        return response_data['response']

# Register listener
vex_listener = VexListener()
EventBus.register('agent_response', vex_listener.on_agent_response)

vexIntegration.title: Memory Growth Monitoring
vexIntegration.description: Prevent unbounded memory expansion with Vex limits
vexIntegration.code:
class VexMemoryGuard(EventListener):
    def on_memory_update(self, memory_state):
        metrics = vex.track_memory_growth(
            session_id=memory_state.session_id,
            token_count=len(memory_state.tokens),
            max_allowed=8000
        )
        
        if metrics.usage_ratio > 0.9:
            # Auto-summarize
            summary = llm.summarize(memory_state.history[-10:])
            memory_state.truncate(summary)
            vex.log_event('memory_truncated', {'tokens_saved': metrics.tokens_saved})
        
        return memory_state

memory_guard = VexMemoryGuard()
EventBus.register('memory_update', memory_guard.on_memory_update)
